page: Microsoft Azure E16s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX, AVX-512F, and AES instructions. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, and includes 262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 13147.43 and a PassMark CPU score of 14224.85. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 99.49 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 57.37 GB/sec for uncached large-block writes. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1246.59 and weak single-threaded compression and decompression results. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the average tier, with Redis achieving 11.37 million operations per second.

Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per core, the E16s_v3 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require top-tier single-core compute speeds. The inclusion of 262 GB of local storage and Premium Storage support makes it suitable for database deployments, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. It is well-suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and general-purpose memory-optimized workloads, though it is less optimal for compute-heavy or single-threaded processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores), 128.0 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. The system delivers average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though its single-core processing and compression speeds are relatively weak. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance provides qualitative resource density for memory-intensive applications. It is best suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and enterprise workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without demanding extreme single-core compute performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 262 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and local storage for database and caching workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
